text.title: pnpm-package-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: No `packageManager` field; pnpm version drifts between devs.
antipattern.symptom: Lockfile churn on every PR; "works on my laptop" install failures.
antipattern.root-cause: No version pin + no corepack activation.
antipattern.fix: Add `"packageManager": "pnpm@9.6.0"` to package.json; run `corepack enable` in CI and onboarding.

antipattern.symptom: Mystery version conflicts between transitive deps; bugs that appear only after `pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile`.
antipattern.root-cause: Hoisting flattens the dep tree and lets undeclared deps resolve accidentally.
antipattern.fix: Set `shamefully-hoist=false`. Add the missing dep declaratively via `.pnpmfile.cjs` readPackage hook, or as a direct devDependency.

antipattern.symptom: CI silently regenerates pnpm-lock.yaml; production deps drift between PRs.
antipattern.root-cause: Default install rewrites the lockfile on small drift; CI never alerts.
antipattern.fix: Replace with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`; fail CI if the lockfile would change.

antipattern.symptom: Every install is a fresh resolution; transitive deps change on every clone.
antipattern.fix: Remove from .gitignore; commit the file; require its presence via pre-commit hook.
